notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. The authoritative specification is the RCA at `docs/feature/fix-noop-self-reenqueue/deliver/bugfix-rca.md` (user-validated 2026-04-29 with explicit approval of Candidate A: filter `Action::Noop` from the `has_work` predicate at the runtime level. Candidate C — changing `NoopHeartbeat` to `vec![]` — was REJECTED on grounds enumerated in the RCA: contradicts ADR-0013 §9 proof-of-life intent, breaks the doctest at `core/reconciler.rs:138`, would require updates to multiple acceptance tests pinning the `vec![Action::Noop]` contract, and would render `Action::Noop` vestigial).

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: the predicate edit lands directly. No deprecation, no shadow predicate, no feature-flagged old path. Candidate A is intentionally surgical — one production file, two edit sites (an import line and the predicate line plus its comment block).

Two-step Outside-In TDD shape, mirroring the prior bugfix at `docs/feature/fix-convergence-loop-not-spawned/deliver/roadmap.json`. Step 01-01 writes the failing regression test (RED — committed clean by marking the test `#[ignore = "RED scaffold for fix-noop-self-reenqueue — un-ignore in the GREEN step"]` so lefthook's nextest-affected pass stays green; the test compiles and is skipped). Step 01-02 lands the predicate fix and removes the `#[ignore]` in the same commit; the test transitions skipped → executed-and-passing.

RED via runtime assertion failure (NOT compile failure). Unlike the prior bugfix (`fix-convergence-loop-not-spawned`) where the RED state was `unknown field` compile errors pinning `ServerConfig.clock` / `ServerConfig.tick_cadence`, this bugfix's regression test exercises only the existing public surface — the runtime's `AppState`, `EvaluationBroker::counters()`, and `run_convergence_tick` are all already in place. The test compiles cleanly against current main; it would FAIL at runtime on `assert_eq!(counters.dispatched, 1)` because pre-fix the counter grows to ≥10 from the `noop-heartbeat` self-re-enqueue. Marking the test `#[ignore]` in step 01-01 keeps the pre-commit lefthook gate (`nextest-affected`) green without forcing `--no-verify`; step 01-02 removes the `#[ignore]` AND the ignored→executed transition is the proof-of-fix.

Test placement: NEW file at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` (default acceptance lane — `tests/acceptance/`, NOT `tests/integration/`). Per ADR-0005 + `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Layout", acceptance tests in this crate are in-process pure-Rust under sim traits; the entrypoint at `tests/acceptance.rs` does NOT carry a `#![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` gate (this crate's acceptance suite stays in the default unit lane per the entrypoint's own header comment lines 12-14: \"Acceptance tests in this crate stay in the default unit lane — they exercise only in-process serde round-trips and utoipa schema emission, no real infrastructure.\"). The new test follows that lane: `Arc<SimClock>`, `Arc<SimDriver(DriverType::Process)>`, `Arc<SimObservationStore>`, `Arc<LocalIntentStore>` on a `tempfile::TempDir`, no axum listener, no reqwest, no real subprocess. Register via `mod runtime_convergence_loop;` inside the existing `mod acceptance { ... }` block at `tests/acceptance.rs:20-35`.

Scope discipline (CRITICAL — user-supplied constraint). This is a focused bugfix: NO surrounding refactors, NO additional tests beyond the regression test, NO comment-only edits to neighbouring code, NO touching files outside the two listed (`reconciler_runtime.rs` for the GREEN edit; `runtime_convergence_loop.rs` + `acceptance.rs` for the test). Per the user's CLAUDE.md guidance: \"A bug fix doesn't need surrounding cleanup.\" The crafter MUST NOT extend the predicate to filter additional sentinel actions, MUST NOT introduce a `Action::is_noop()` helper, MUST NOT add new test cases against `JobLifecycle` mixed-action behavior beyond what the regression test covers.

Out of scope (tracked separately, NOT a step in this roadmap). The RCA flagged that adding a DST invariant in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` asserting \"after K ticks against a converged cluster, `broker.dispatched` is bounded by the number of distinct edge-triggered submits\" would have caught this pre-merge under DST replay. That work is OUT OF SCOPE for this roadmap. Do not add it as a step. The reviewer's hint will be tracked as a separate follow-up.

Mutation gate compatibility (note for the GREEN step). The line-256 predicate edit produces a small, well-defined mutation surface: the canonical mutants cargo-mutants will generate include (a) replacing `actions.iter().any(|a| !matches!(a, Action::Noop))` with `false` (every reconciler-emitted action is suppressed), (b) replacing it with `true` (current bugged behaviour: any non-empty actions vec re-enqueues), (c) flipping the `!matches!` to `matches!` (only Noop-emitting reconcilers re-enqueue — inverted semantics). The regression test's two assertions (`counters.dispatched == 1` AND `counters.queued == 0`) jointly kill (b) and (c); (a) is killed by the existing `runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs` test which asserts on observable broker activity. Run `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane` (no `--features integration-tests` flag — the acceptance suite is in the default lane and no integration tests participate) to verify ≥80% kill rate.

macOS-vs-Linux invocation hygiene per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running integration tests locally on macOS — Lima VM": this regression test lives in the default acceptance lane (NOT behind `--features integration-tests`), so `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` runs natively on macOS without Lima. The Lima requirement applies only to commands that pass `--features integration-tests` or `--features integration-tests` to mutants. Both quality-gate sections below distinguish: default-lane invocations run natively; if a future invocation reaches into integration-tests it MUST go through `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS.

Standing user-rule references the crafter must obey: (1) `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` — no shims, no deprecations. (2) `feedback_no_git_stash` — focused-subset commits use `git add <paths>` + `git restore --staged`, never `git stash`. (3) `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer` — clippy issues that surface during the work get fixed in-scope. (4) `feedback_no_rename_stubs` — no leftover stubs. (5) `feedback_delete_dont_gate` — no `#[cfg(test)]` annotations to silence dead-code warnings. (6) `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running tests — foreground, always" — every test invocation goes through `Bash` with `run_in_background: false` except the post-GREEN mutation pass.

Roadmap-validator length warnings (`STEP_NAME_TOO_LONG`, `CRITERIA_TOO_LONG`, `DESCRIPTION_TOO_LONG`) are non-blocking per CLAUDE.md \"Roadmap validator warnings\" — Overdrive convention deliberately carries scenario-level specificity in ACs (test names, predicate shape, line numbers, gate predicates). Do NOT trim ACs to satisfy these warnings.

reviews.scope: RCA + proposed fix shape (Candidate A: filter `Action::Noop` from the runtime's `has_work` predicate. Candidate C — changing `NoopHeartbeat` to return `vec![]` — was explicitly rejected on grounds of ADR-0013 §9 proof-of-life intent, doctest fallout, multi-test contract churn, and rendering `Action::Noop` vestigial).
reviews.notes: User confirmed root cause (`NoopHeartbeat`'s contract — `vec![Action::Noop]` — is encoded as syntactic non-emptiness, but the §18 level-triggered re-enqueue gate operates on syntactic emptiness, missing the documented \"nothing to do this tick\" semantic). User APPROVED Candidate A specifically over Candidate C on the architectural grounds enumerated in the RCA. User APPROVED the regression-test design (single submit against converged AppState, drive 10 ticks, assert `dispatched == 1` and `queued == 0`). Out of scope explicitly: DST invariant addition in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` (separate follow-up); any other surrounding refactor.

phases.name: Filter `Action::Noop` from the level-triggered re-enqueue gate so `NoopHeartbeat` honors its documented \"nothing to do this tick\" semantic
phases.description: Two-step Outside-In TDD bugfix. Step 01-01 creates a NEW acceptance test file at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` and registers it under `tests/acceptance.rs:20-35`'s `mod acceptance { ... }` block. The test builds a converged-state `AppState` (sim observation/intent/driver, IntentStore preloaded with one job whose `desired.replicas == actual.allocations.running.count`), submits ONE `Evaluation { reconciler: "job-lifecycle", target: "job/<id>" }` to the broker, drives the convergence loop manually for 10 ticks (drain pending → call `run_convergence_tick` per drained eval), and asserts `state.runtime.broker().counters().dispatched == 1` AND `state.runtime.broker().counters().queued == 0`. Pre-fix, `dispatched` grows to ≥10 because `noop-heartbeat` self-re-enqueues on every tick (the documented bug); the test would FAIL on the `dispatched == 1` assertion against current `main`. The test is committed marked `#[ignore = \"RED scaffold for fix-noop-self-reenqueue — un-ignore in the GREEN step\"]` so the pre-commit lefthook gate (`nextest-affected`) stays green without `--no-verify`. Step 01-02 applies the Candidate A fix in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs`: (a) add `Action` to the existing `overdrive_core::reconciler::{...}` import at lines 36-39; (b) replace line 256 `let has_work = !actions.is_empty();` with `let has_work = actions.iter().any(|a| !matches!(a, Action::Noop));`; (c) update the comment block at lines 250-255 to explain WHY `Action::Noop` is filtered (it is the documented \"nothing to do this tick\" sentinel per `core/reconciler.rs:447`; `action_shim::dispatch` already treats it as a no-op per `action_shim.rs:108`; the §18 level-triggered re-enqueue gate must honor that documented semantic). The same step ALSO removes the `#[ignore]` attribute from the regression test added in Step 01-01. Single-cut migration: the production edit + test un-ignore land in one cohesive commit. The regression test transitions ignored → executed-and-passing within Step 01-02's commit. All existing acceptance and DST tests must remain GREEN. `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane`, `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`, and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` all green.

steps.name: RED regression test — `noop-heartbeat` against converged target must not self-re-enqueue across 10 ticks
steps.description: Create a NEW acceptance test file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` and register it under the existing `tests/acceptance.rs:20-35` `mod acceptance { ... }` block. The new file does NOT carry a `#![cfg(feature = \"integration-tests\")]` attribute — this crate's acceptance suite is in the default unit lane per the `tests/acceptance.rs` header comment (lines 12-14). Test fixture pattern follows `tests/acceptance/runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs:21-44` for the import surface (`Arc<SimClock>`, `Arc<SimDriver(DriverType::Process)>`, `Arc<SimObservationStore>`, `Arc<LocalIntentStore>`). New test function: `#[tokio::test] #[ignore = \"RED scaffold for fix-noop-self-reenqueue — un-ignore in the GREEN step\"] async fn noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue() { ... }`. Body shape: (1) Construct sim adapters: `let clock = Arc::new(SimClock::new());`, `let obs: Arc<dyn ObservationStore> = Arc::new(SimObservationStore::single_peer(NodeId::new(\"local\").expect(\"node id\"), 0));`, `let driver: Arc<dyn Driver> = Arc::new(SimDriver::new(DriverType::Process));`, `let tmp = TempDir::new().expect(\"tempdir\");`. (2) Construct `let store: Arc<dyn IntentStore> = Arc::new(LocalIntentStore::open(tmp.path().join(\"intent.redb\")).expect(\"intent store\"));`. (3) Construct `let runtime = Arc::new(ReconcilerRuntime::new(tmp.path().join(\"reconciler\"), clock.clone()).expect(\"runtime\"));`. (4) Build `AppState::new(store, obs, runtime, driver)`. (5) Preload IntentStore with one converged job: `Job { id: JobId::new(\"payments\")?, replicas: 1, ... }` written via `state.store.put(IntentKey::Job(...), ...)`; preload observation with one Running alloc against that job (the `runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs` test's helper pattern is the reference shape). (6) Submit ONE Evaluation: `state.runtime.broker().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: ReconcilerName::new(\"job-lifecycle\")?, target: TargetResource::new(\"job/payments\")? });`. (7) Drive 10 convergence ticks: `for tick_n in 0..10u64 { let now = clock.now(); let deadline = now + Duration::from_millis(100); let pending = state.runtime.broker().drain_pending(); for eval in pending { let _ = run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.target, now, tick_n, deadline).await; } clock.tick(Duration::from_millis(100)); }`. NOTE: `state.runtime.broker()` returns a guard with `.submit(...)` and `.drain_pending(...)` and `.counters()` already accessible — no `&mut` required (verified at `reconciler_runtime.rs:264-275` where the production self-re-enqueue path uses the same shape). (8) Assertion 1 (kills the bugged behaviour — `noop-heartbeat` grows the dispatched counter on every tick): `let counters = state.runtime.broker().counters(); assert_eq!(counters.dispatched, 1, \"noop-heartbeat against a converged target must not self-re-enqueue; expected dispatched == 1, got {}\", counters.dispatched);`. Pre-fix value: ≥10. Post-fix value: 1 (the original edge-triggered submit is dispatched once and `noop-heartbeat`'s `vec![Action::Noop]` no longer trips the re-enqueue gate). (9) Assertion 2 (kills the inverted-predicate mutation): `assert_eq!(counters.queued, 0, \"convergence must complete with no pending evaluations; got {}\", counters.queued);`. (10) RED verification: against current main, the test is `#[ignore]`d so `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` skips it (and lefthook's pre-commit gate stays green); manually un-ignoring locally and running it would fail on assertion 1. Step 01-02 removes the `#[ignore]` and the test runs. The crafter MUST NOT add behavior to `runtime_convergence_loop.rs` beyond this single test function; this file exists exclusively as the regression-test holder for the bug. No production-code edits in this step. Sub-task: append `mod runtime_convergence_loop;` inside the existing `mod acceptance { ... }` block at `tests/acceptance.rs:20-35` (the `mod` declarations are alphabetically ordered there — insert in the correct alphabetical position between `pending_no_capacity_renders_reason` and `row_body_conversions`). Commit message: `test(reconciler): pin noop-heartbeat must not self-re-enqueue under converged target (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit goes through normal pre-commit hooks (no `--no-verify` because the test is `#[ignore]`d and lefthook's nextest-affected pass passes).
steps.criteria: New file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` with one `#[tokio::test] #[ignore = \"RED scaffold for fix-noop-self-reenqueue — un-ignore in the GREEN step\"] async fn noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue()` (and no other tests). The body builds a converged AppState fixture, submits exactly one `(reconciler=\"job-lifecycle\", target=\"job/payments\")` Evaluation, drives 10 ticks of `run_convergence_tick`, and asserts both `counters.dispatched == 1` AND `counters.queued == 0`. `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance.rs:20-35` is appended with `mod runtime_convergence_loop;` inside `mod acceptance { ... }` in correct alphabetical position. The new file does NOT carry a `#![cfg(...)]` attribute and does NOT carry `#[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]` (sim traits are platform-independent). RED verification: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0 with the test reported as ignored; if the `#[ignore]` is manually removed locally, the test fails at assertion 1 (`dispatched == 1` against pre-fix `dispatched ≥ 10`). No production-code files touched (`git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only the new test file and the modified `tests/acceptance.rs`). No new feature flags introduced. Commit message: `test(reconciler): pin noop-heartbeat must not self-re-enqueue under converged target (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Defect (closure pin) — the new test directly demonstrates the user-observable bug: pre-fix, after one edge-triggered Evaluation against a converged AppState, `broker.dispatched` grows by ≥1 per tick because `noop-heartbeat`'s `vec![Action::Noop]` trips the syntactic-emptiness `has_work` predicate. The test pins the post-fix expected value (1) and the post-fix expected pending-queue depth (0).

RCA §Root cause closure — the test exercises the level-triggered re-enqueue gate at `reconciler_runtime.rs:256` directly: a passing assertion 1 (`dispatched == 1`) is only achievable when `has_work` returns false for `vec![Action::Noop]`, which is exactly Candidate A's semantics.

RCA §Fix surface — Candidate A — the test pins the predicate's required behaviour: `noop-heartbeat` emits `vec![Action::Noop]` on every tick (verified at `core/reconciler.rs:773-775`); under Candidate A's `actions.iter().any(|a| !matches!(a, Action::Noop))` predicate, the iter-any returns false, `has_work` is false, no self-re-enqueue happens, and `dispatched` stays at 1.

RCA §Risk closure — `JobLifecycle::reconcile` is the only other registered reconciler. The test fixture's preloaded converged state guarantees `JobLifecycle` returns `(Vec::new(), view.clone())` per `core/reconciler.rs:1089-1203` lines 1089+; with empty actions, `has_work` is false under both pre-fix and post-fix predicates, so `JobLifecycle` correctly does not re-enqueue. The test isolates the `noop-heartbeat`-specific pathology.

RCA §Regression test design — the test is named `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` per the RCA's specification; lives at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` (default acceptance lane, no integration-tests gate); fails against current code if un-ignored; passes after the GREEN step lands.

Test placement per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Layout": new file under `tests/acceptance/`, registered through the existing `tests/acceptance.rs` entrypoint's inline `mod acceptance { ... }` block. The acceptance suite stays in the default unit lane per the entrypoint header comment.

RED verified at runtime: pre-fix the test would fail on `assert_eq!(counters.dispatched, 1, ...)` (actual: ≥10). The test is `#[ignore]`d in this commit so lefthook's nextest-affected pass stays green; the GREEN step removes the `#[ignore]` and the runtime assertion passes.

The crafter does NOT introduce additional test cases beyond `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue`, does NOT add helper modules, does NOT modify `runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs` or any other existing test, and does NOT introduce a shared fixture file. The new file holds one function and only the imports it requires.
No production-code edits (production fix lands in 01-02). No new feature flags introduced. The only files modified are the new test file and the `tests/acceptance.rs` mod-declaration append.
Commit goes through normal pre-commit hooks (no `--no-verify`). Lefthook nextest-affected pass passes because the test is `#[ignore]`d. Commit message carries `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer.
steps.tier: Tier 1 (DST/acceptance — default unit lane; sim traits only, no real I/O, runs on macOS and Linux uniformly).

Default-lane test pass with the new test reported as ignored: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; the test summary explicitly shows `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` as ignored.
Workspace-wide default lane stays green: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0.
Clippy clean: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0 on `overdrive-control-plane`.
No production-code paths touched: `git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only the new test file and the modified `tests/acceptance.rs`.
No `#![cfg(...)]` attribute on the new test file (the entrypoint does not gate the binary; the test is platform-independent because it uses sim adapters).
No new env-var / cwd / process-global mutation introduced — `#[serial_test::serial(env)]` is NOT needed; the test uses tempdir-scoped state.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit goes through normal pre-commit hooks (no `--no-verify`).
deliverables: New file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` with one `#[tokio::test] #[ignore = \"RED scaffold for fix-noop-self-reenqueue — un-ignore in the GREEN step\"]` named `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue`.
`crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance.rs` updated with `mod runtime_convergence_loop;` inside the existing `mod acceptance { ... }` block (lines 20-35) in correct alphabetical position.
Single commit with normal pre-commit hooks and `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer.
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE EXECUTED — re-read RCA §Defect, §Fix surface, §Risk, and §Regression test design. Audit existing fixtures: (a) `tests/acceptance/runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs:21-44` for the canonical sim-adapter import surface (`SimClock`, `SimDriver(DriverType::Process)`, `SimObservationStore::single_peer`, `LocalIntentStore`, `ReconcilerRuntime::new`, `AppState::new`). (b) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs:264-275` for the broker-submit shape (no `&mut` needed; the broker accessor returns a guard with `.submit(...)`/`.drain_pending(...)`/`.counters()` directly available). (c) `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:773-775` for `NoopHeartbeat::reconcile` returning `vec![Action::Noop]` unconditionally — this is the bug surface the test pins. RED_ACCEPTANCE EXECUTED — write the test. Imports needed: `std::sync::Arc`, `std::time::Duration`, `overdrive_control_plane::reconciler_runtime::{ReconcilerRuntime, run_convergence_tick}`, `overdrive_control_plane::eval_broker::Evaluation`, `overdrive_control_plane::AppState`, `overdrive_core::aggregate::{IntentKey, Job}`, `overdrive_core::id::{JobId, NodeId}`, `overdrive_core::reconciler::{ReconcilerName, TargetResource}`, `overdrive_core::traits::driver::{Driver, DriverType}`, `overdrive_core::traits::observation_store::ObservationStore`, `overdrive_core::traits::intent_store::IntentStore`, `overdrive_sim::adapters::clock::SimClock`, `overdrive_sim::adapters::driver::SimDriver`, `overdrive_sim::adapters::observation_store::SimObservationStore`, `overdrive_store_local::LocalIntentStore`, `tempfile::TempDir`. RED_UNIT SKIPPED — the test exercises only the runtime's existing public surface; no internal helper introduced. GREEN SKIPPED — production fix lands in Step 01-02. COMMIT EXECUTED — `git commit` (no `--no-verify`) with body explaining: this is a RED scaffold; the test is `#[ignore]`d for the lefthook gate; Step 01-02 removes the `#[ignore]` and lands the predicate fix; the test transitions ignored → executed-and-passing in Step 01-02's commit. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` — do NOT edit `execution-log.json` directly. CRITICAL crafter callout: the SimClock method is `tick(&self, duration: Duration)` (sync, returns `()`) — verified at `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/clock.rs:55`. Use the literal call `clock.tick(Duration::from_millis(100));` per loop iteration. The test's broker accessor pattern matches the production self-re-enqueue at `reconciler_runtime.rs:271-275`: `state.runtime.broker().submit(...)`, `state.runtime.broker().drain_pending()`, `state.runtime.broker().counters()` — all without `&mut`.

steps.name: GREEN minimal fix — filter `Action::Noop` from the level-triggered re-enqueue gate at `reconciler_runtime.rs:256`
steps.description: Apply the RCA §Approved fix Candidate A in a single cohesive commit. THREE edits in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` plus ONE edit in the regression test. (1) Add `Action` to the existing `overdrive_core::reconciler::{...}` import at lines 36-39 (the import currently reads `use overdrive_core::reconciler::{ AnyReconciler, AnyReconcilerView, AnyState, JobLifecycleState, JobLifecycleView, LibsqlHandle, ReconcilerName, TargetResource, TickContext, };` — add `Action,` to that list, alphabetically sorted before `AnyReconciler`). (2) Replace line 256 `let has_work = !actions.is_empty();` with `let has_work = actions.iter().any(|a| !matches!(a, Action::Noop));`. (3) Update the comment block at lines 250-255 (currently explains the consume-by-value ordering only) so it ALSO documents WHY `Action::Noop` is filtered: the new comment block must (a) preserve the existing rationale about consume-by-value ordering with `action_shim::dispatch`, AND (b) add a paragraph explaining that `Action::Noop` is the documented \"nothing to do this tick\" sentinel per `core/reconciler.rs:447`, that `action_shim::dispatch` already treats it as a no-op per `action_shim.rs:108`, and that the §18 level-triggered re-enqueue gate must honor that documented semantic by treating an all-Noop actions vec as semantically empty. The why-not-what comment shape per `CLAUDE.md` discipline. (4) In `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs`, REMOVE the `#[ignore = \"...\"]` attribute from `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue`. The test transitions skipped → executed-and-passing within this commit. Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: the predicate edit + import + comment + un-ignore land in one cohesive commit. No deprecation, no shadow predicate, no two-phase rollout. Commit message: `fix(reconciler): filter Action::Noop from has_work re-enqueue gate (Candidate A — see RCA at docs/feature/fix-noop-self-reenqueue/deliver/bugfix-rca.md)` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production changes (single cohesive commit) — exactly the 3 edits enumerated in the description: (1) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` imports add `Action` to the `overdrive_core::reconciler::{...}` list at lines 36-39. (2) Line 256 reads `let has_work = actions.iter().any(|a| !matches!(a, Action::Noop));` (literal predicate shape; verified by `git grep -n 'has_work = ' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs`). (3) The comment block at lines 250-255 documents WHY `Action::Noop` is filtered (sentinel semantic per `core/reconciler.rs:447`; `action_shim::dispatch` already no-ops it per `action_shim.rs:108`; level-triggered gate must honor the documented semantic). (4) The regression test from Step 01-01 has its `#[ignore]` attribute removed; the test now runs in the default lane. (2) `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0 with `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` reported as PASS (not ignored). acceptance_criteria: RCA §Defect (closure) — `has_work` at `reconciler_runtime.rs:256` no longer trips on `vec![Action::Noop]`. The `noop-heartbeat` reconciler's documented \"nothing to do this tick\" semantic (per `core/reconciler.rs:447`) is now honored by the level-triggered §18 re-enqueue gate.

RCA §Root cause (closure) — the predicate now operates on the documented semantic (any non-Noop action means real work) rather than syntactic emptiness. `vec![Action::Noop]` is treated as semantically empty; `vec![Action::StartAllocation { ... }]` and `vec![Action::Noop, Action::RestartAllocation { ... }]` correctly trigger the re-enqueue.

RCA §Fix surface — Candidate A — exactly the 3 edits in `reconciler_runtime.rs` land: import addition, predicate replacement, comment-block extension. No additional edits to the file. No `Action::is_noop()` helper introduced (the `matches!` macro is the canonical Rust shape).

steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE EXECUTED — re-read RCA §Fix surface — Candidate A and §Why Candidate C was rejected. Confirm current line numbers in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` (36-39 for the `overdrive_core::reconciler::{...}` import; 250-256 for the comment block + `has_work` line; 260-275 for the dispatch + self-re-enqueue block). Locate by symbol name and grep, not by line number — the file may have drifted slightly. Confirm the existing imports list at lines 36-39 to determine the correct alphabetical insertion point for `Action,` (alphabetically between nothing and `AnyReconciler`, so it becomes the first item in the list). RED_ACCEPTANCE EXECUTED — Step 01-01 already wrote the regression test; verify it currently runs as ignored (`cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` summary shows the test as ignored). RED_UNIT SKIPPED — 1-line predicate change; no new internal helper introduced. GREEN EXECUTED — implement in this order: (1) Edit `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` imports at lines 36-39: add `Action,` as the first item in the `use overdrive_core::reconciler::{ ... };` braces (so the new line reads `use overdrive_core::reconciler::{ Action, AnyReconciler, AnyReconcilerView, AnyState, JobLifecycleState, JobLifecycleView, LibsqlHandle, ReconcilerName, TargetResource, TickContext, };`). (2) Edit line 256: replace `let has_work = !actions.is_empty();` with `let has_work = actions.iter().any(|a| !matches!(a, Action::Noop));`. (3) Extend the comment block at lines 250-255: keep the existing 6 lines explaining the consume-by-value ordering with `action_shim::dispatch`, and append a new paragraph (3-4 lines) explaining: `Action::Noop` is the documented \"nothing to do this tick\" sentinel (`core/reconciler.rs:447`), `action_shim::dispatch` already treats it as a no-op (`action_shim.rs:108`), and the §18 level-triggered re-enqueue gate must honor that documented semantic by treating an all-Noop actions vec as semantically empty. (4) Run `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` — should compile clean. (5) Edit `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs`: remove the `#[ignore = \"RED scaffold for fix-noop-self-reenqueue — un-ignore in the GREEN step\"]` attribute on the `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` test. The test signature now reads `#[tokio::test] async fn noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue() { ... }`. (6) Run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -E 'test(noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue)'` — the test should pass; assertion 1 reports `counters.dispatched == 1` and assertion 2 reports `counters.queued == 0`. (7) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace` — full default lane stays green; all existing tests pinning the `vec![Action::Noop]` contract on the reconciler side continue to pass (`runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs`, `reconciler_trait_surface.rs`, `any_reconciler_dispatch.rs`). (8) Run `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` — green; the doctest at `core/reconciler.rs:138` exercises the type signature, not the broker semantics. (9) Run `cargo xtask dst` — green. (10) Run `cargo xtask dst-lint` — green. (11) Run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` on `overdrive-control-plane` — green. COMMIT EXECUTED — `git commit` (no `--no-verify`); commit body explicitly enumerates the 3 production edits + test un-ignore; cites the RCA path. Single cohesive commit. POST-GREEN MUTATION RUN: per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing exception, mutation runs use `run_in_background: true`. Invoke `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane` (NO `--features integration-tests` flag — the regression test is in the default acceptance lane); wait for completion via the shell ID; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` for the kill rate. Expected mutations to be killed (3 distinct classes): (a) replacing the predicate body with `false` (every reconciler emission suppressed) — killed by `runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs` (the proof-of-life invariant requires observable broker activity); (b) replacing with `true` (the original bugged behaviour) — killed by the new test's `counters.dispatched == 1` assertion (would observe ≥10); (c) flipping `!matches!` to `matches!` (only Noop emitters re-enqueue, real work suppressed) — killed by both the new test's `dispatched == 1` (no real reconciler emits Noop with non-empty companions in the converged fixture, so dispatched would be 0 under flipped predicate) AND `queued == 0` assertions. On any surviving mutation, add a concrete test case that disambiguates it AND re-run mutation — NOT extend predicate scope beyond `Action::Noop`, NOT weaken existing assertions. Always run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase`. Post-commit smoke check: re-run `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane`, `cargo xtask dst`, and `cargo xtask dst-lint` one more time to confirm zero regressions; this is the input to Phase 3.5 post-merge integration gate.
